items.title: Prosecution closes case against former Perlis MB after 21 witnesses testify
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The prosecution today closed its case in the Sessions Court here after calling 21 witnesses to testify in the trial involving former Perlis Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Azlan Man, who faces 10 charges related to submitting false claims and receiving proceeds from unlawful activities. The trial, which began on July 18, 2024, heard evidence from several witnesses including former Sri Kedawang Travel & Tour (W) Sdn Bhd (SKTT) managing director Shafiei Ismail, former secretary for both the Perlis State Legislative Assembly and the Perlis State Executive Council Norazlan Yahya, former senior private secretary to Azlan, Herwardy Hashim; and Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) investigating officer Mohamad Safwan Arbangi. The former Bintong Assemblyman also faces five charges of allegedly receiving RM1.06 million in proceeds from unlawful activities involving payments linked to SKTT, Aidil Travel & Tours and Abdul Rahim Halim, who represented Aidil Travel.

items.title: New mosque to rise in front of TRX as Bukit Bintang seeks permanent worship space
items.summary: Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Zulkifli Hassan said the initiative reflects efforts to strengthen Islamic facilities in the Bukit Bintang area, Malay daily Kosmo! He said the project involves a 5.6-acre site located opposite TRX. Zulkifli said the project is expected to become a new landmark in Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: International trips lift MAHB's passenger traffic to 7.9mil in May
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) recorded 7.9 million passenger movements across its network in May 2026, supported by sustained international travel demand. In a statement, the airport operator said Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) handled 4.9 million passengers during the month. Of KLIA's total passenger traffic, 3.6 million were international travellers, representing a two per cent increase compared to May 2025.

items.title: Two hurt in collision involving three-tonne, five-tonne lorries
items.summary: NIBONG TEBAL: Two lorry drivers were injured after their vehicles collided along Jalan Taman Kuang Indah in Sungai Jawi near here this morning. Penang Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations John Sagun Francis said firefighters received an emergency call on the incident at 7.04am. "The accident involved a three-tonne Hino lorry and a five-tonne Hino lorry.

items.title: Teacher among four arrested on suspicion of using Besut house as drug den
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — Police have arrested four men, including a teacher, after raiding a house suspected of being used for drug-related activities in Kampung Raja, Besut early yesterday. According to Sinar Harian , Besut police chief Superintendent Mohd Rozaime Ab Rahim said the raid was carried out at about 1.30am by the Besut district police headquarters’ Narcotics Criminal Investigation Division following intelligence on suspected drug distribution in the area. Mohd Rozaime said initial urine screening found that three of the four suspects tested positive for Tetrahydrocannabinol, or THC.

items.title: MACC has legal discretion over compounds, says Chief Justice
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Chief Justice Tun Wan Ahmad Farid Wan Salleh said the use of compounds and settlements in corruption-related cases falls within the prerogative of enforcement agencies. He said enforcement bodies, including the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC), are empowered by law to exercise such discretion. Wan Ahmad Farid was responding to concerns over the increasing use of compounds and charge withdrawals in corruption-related cases.

items.title: MACC, government win appeal to overturn RM900k malicious process judgment
items.summary: A three-member Court of Appeal bench comprising Justices Datuk Azhahari Kamal Ramli, Datuk Ahmad Kamal Md Shahid, and Datuk Nadzarin Wok Nordin unanimously allowed the appeals, setting aside the High Court's decisions dated Aug 25, 2023, on liability, and May 28, 2024, on the quantum of damages awarded to Nik Mohd Suhaimi Ahmad Ghazali. The appellate court ruled that there was no legal basis for imposing liability on MACC under the tort of malicious process, particularly after the High Court had dismissed Nik Mohd Suhaimi's separate claim for malicious prosecution. The Court of Appeal also ordered Nik Mohd Suhaimi to pay RM90,000 in costs.

items.title: Anwar cleared from testifying as witness in RM30mil lawsuit against Vinod Sekhar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court has allowed an application by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim to set aside an order requiring him to appear as a subpoenaed witness in a RM30 million civil suit. The suit was brought by a dozen people against businessman Datuk Vinod Balachandra Sekhar and his wife. In allowing Anwar's application to set aside the subpoena issued on Jan 15, High Court judicial commissioner Datuk Muhammad Adam @ Edward Abdullah ruled that the plaintiffs had failed to establish that the prime minister was relevant or material witness in the case and his attendance was necessary.

items.title: Low water levels at major dams trigger cloud seeding and fire warning in Sarawak
items.summary: KUCHING, June 19 — The water level at Batang Ai Dam is currently at ‘Event and Drought Emergency Level 1’, while Bakun Dam is at ‘Drought Emergency Level 1’, revealed Datuk Amar Douglas Uggah Embas. He said the Sarawak government has launched another round of cloud seeding operations in collaboration with the National Disaster Management Agency (Nadma) focussing on major dams and water catchment areas across the state. Uggah, who is also State Disaster Management Committee (SDMC) chairman, said a similar operation conducted in May had successfully increased water levels at Batang Ai Dam, although water levels at Bakun Dam remained a concern.

items.title: UNHCR cardholders not immune to Malaysian laws, says Immigration DG
items.summary: PORT DICKSON: United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) cardholders are not immune to enforcement action and remain subject to Malaysian laws if they commit offences. Immigration Department director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said while valid UNHCR card holders are not detained for immigration documentation offences after verification, they still face action for violating other laws. He said this after the passing-out parade for the Basic Immigration Certificate Course and Advanced Immigration Certificate Course Series 1/2026, involving 124 officers here today.

items.title: Chinese citizen fined RM5,000 for mid-flight theft attempt
items.summary: SEPANG: A Chinese citizen was fined RM5,000 by the magistrate's court after pleading guilty to attempting to steal a Japanese passenger's backpack during a flight from Timor-Leste to Kuala Lumpur earlier this week. After the proceedings, Hu settled the fine. Hu was charged with attempting to steal a backpack belonging to a 58-year-old Japanese passenger aboard a flight from Dili at 12.59pm on Monday.

items.title: Liftech's IPO oversubscribed by 18.9 times
items.summary: The company received 5,636 applications for 314.79 million shares from the Malaysian public, compared with the 15.8 million shares offered. Under the listing exercise, Liftech will issue 79.23 million new shares at an issue price of 29 sen each, alongside an offer for sale of 15.8 million existing shares to selected investors through private placement. Based on its enlarged share capital of 314.94 million shares, Liftech is expected to have a market capitalisation of RM91.33 million upon listing.

items.title: Cheras student who choked schoolmate over canteen seat suspended
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A student who allegedly assaulted his schoolmate at a Cheras secondary school in April has been suspended from school. The assailant, police said, allegedly choked the victim's neck from behind and verbally provoked him. "Police received a report on April 16," Cheras police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Rosdi Daud said in a statement.

items.title: High Court sets aside subpoena for Anwar to testify in RM30m civil suit against businessman and wife
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — The High Court here has set aside a subpoena requiring Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim to appear as a witness in a RM30 million civil suit filed by 12 individuals against businessman Datuk Vinod Balachandra Sekhar and his wife, Winny Yeap Liew Heoh. Judicial Commissioner Datuk Muhammad Adam @ Edward Abdullah, in allowing Anwar’s application to set aside the subpoena issued on January 15, ruled that the plaintiffs had failed to establish that the Prime Minister was a relevant and material witness to the proceedings. “The plaintiffs relied on photographs, social media postings and public appearances involving Anwar and Vinod.

items.title: National Journalists' Day: Celebrating the keepers of truth
items.summary: LETTER: JOURNALISTS are the eyes and ears of our society. Long before the Internet existed, their hard work kept us updated on our nation's progress and major events. Today's reporters work in a fast-moving digital environment, shaped largely by social media.

items.title: 'Dismantle the stigma': Govt rolls out five-year mental health plan for civil servants
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Public Service Department (PSD) today launched its Human Resources Psychology Services Strategic Plan 2026-2030, a five-year roadmap aimed at strengthening the psychological well-being of civil servants. The plan outlines 12 strategies, 22 programmes and 48 key performance indicators (KPIs) to enhance mental health support across the public service. PSD said the strategic plan was launched during its June 2026 monthly assembly, themed "R&R (Rest and Treat) Your Soul", which was officiated by Public Service director-general Tan Sri Wan Ahmad Dahlan Abdul Aziz.

items.title: MICCI calls for greater resilience, agility in tougher global landscape
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Businesses must strengthen resilience, adaptability and public-private cooperation as they navigate a more complex global operating environment, said the Malaysian International Chamber of Commerce and Industry (MICCI). The operating landscape is being shaped by geopolitical tensions, supply chain disruptions and shifting economic conditions, MICCI said during its 53rd annual general meeting (AGM) and annual luncheon yesterday. It has become the operating environment," Tee said.

items.title: TA Securities initiates coverage on DNeX with 'Buy' call
items.summary: "Earnings are expected to grow further to RM135.3 million in FY27 and RM166.7 million in FY28," it said. TA Securities said earnings growth is expected to be driven by SilTerra's capacity expansion, improving utilisation rates and continued growth in average selling prices from richer product mix. TA Securities said SilTerra's outlook remains favourable, supported by strong growth potential in the silicon photonics segment, which is expected to be a key long-term growth driver amid robust demand from AI and data centre applications.

items.title: Teacher among four arrested in drug raid
items.summary: BESUT: A teacher was among four men arrested for suspected cannabis distribution following a raid at a house in Kampung Raja yesterday. District police chief Superintendent Mohd Rozaime Ab Rahim said the 1.30am raid was conducted based on intelligence regarding drug trafficking in the area. He said police found 21.70g of suspected cannabis, valued at RM1,085, during the inspection.

items.title: Four-vehicle crash leaves motorcyclist injured in George Town
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: A motorcyclist was injured in a four-vehicle crash involving three cars and a motorcycle at the Lorong Batu Lanchang-Thean Teik Highway junction yesterday. Northeast district police chief, Assistant Commissioner Abdul Rozak Muhammad, said preliminary investigations found that three vehicles — two cars and a motorcycle — were travelling along Lorong Batu Lanchang when the first car allegedly lost control and rammed into the rear of the second car and the motorcycle. "The driver of the first car, a 22-year-old man from Johor, tested negative for alcohol and drugs.

items.title: Chief Justice: Public trust is judiciary’s most valuable asset, must be earned
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — Public trust remains the judiciary’s most valuable asset and must be earned through integrity, accountability and consistent conduct, said Chief Justice Tun Wan Ahmad Farid Wan Salleh. Drawing on the legacy of former Chief Justice Tun Zaki Azmi, he said institutional leaders must be prepared to make difficult decisions and work to leave behind stronger, more trusted institutions for future generations. Elaborating on the objectives of the lecture series, he said the Tun Zaki Lectures on Leadership and Stewardship and the Leadership and Stewardship Research Initiative were intended to promote meaningful dialogue on leadership and develop practical scholarship relevant to the realities of institutional leadership.

items.title: Court sentences grandfather to 33 years jail, 14 strokes for raping and sexually assaulting his granddaughters
items.summary: The man, who has three children, was accused in the first and fourth charges of committing sexual assault against his 12-year-old granddaughter at a house in Batu Pahat. The offences were framed under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, read together with Section 16 of the same Act. The fifth charge involved the sexual assault of another granddaughter, aged nine, at the same location last year.

items.title: SIS Forum plans bill to tackle unpaid spousal, child support
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Non-governmental organisation SIS Forum is set to collaborate with key stakeholders to draft a comprehensive proposal bill aimed at ensuring people do not evade their financial responsibilities toward their spouses, ex-spouses, and children. This initiative follows data from Telenisa, SIS Forum's free legal advice service which specifically helps people navigate Islamic family law. According to Telenisa Statistics and Findings 2025 report published yesterday, 1,000 of those cases involved child maintenance (367 cases) and its arrears (633 cases).

items.title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain warning issued for several states until 5pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning with heavy rain and strong winds expected in several areas across Peninsular Malaysia, Sabah and Sarawak until 5pm today. In a weather warning issued this afternoon, MetMalaysia said the entire states of Perlis, Penang and Perak are expected to be affected by adverse weather conditions. In Kedah, the warning covers Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Yan, Kuala Muda, Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu.

items.title: Notice on Hamzah as opposition leader to be sent before next parliament sitting
items.summary: "The notification to the Speaker regarding Hamzah's appointment as opposition leader will be conveyed before the next Dewan Rakyat sitting. "The opposition leader is decided by opposition MPs, who choose among themselves a person who receives majority support from opposition lawmakers. "All opposition MPs, except six, agreed that Hamzah should be the opposition leader.

items.title: PKR leaves Rafizi, Nik Nazmi matter to legal team
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: PKR has left any decision on possible action against former Pandan MP Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and former Setiawangsa MP Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad to its legal team following their departure from the party. Secretary-general Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh said the party's lawyers would determine the next course of action involving the former PKR deputy president and former vice-president. On May 17, Rafizi and Nik Nazmi vacated their parliamentary seats before announcing their exit from PKR.

items.title: PN Supreme Council to meet June 22 over logo use, candidate endorsements
items.summary: PN information chief Tan Sri Annuar Musa said matters involving Pas would be decided by the party itself, while issues concerning PN must be deliberated and resolved at the coalition level. "Anything related to Pas is decided by Pas but matters concerning PN must be decided through PN meetings. "We cannot rush into holding a PN meeting.

items.title: Malaysia, global firms ramp up AI use across key business units
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Companies globally, including those in Malaysia, are accelerating their adoption of artificial intelligence (AI), as businesses increasingly deploy the technology to reshape operations, improve efficiency and unlock new growth opportunities. "In Malaysia, companies across financial services, healthcare, manufacturing and logistics are steadily deploying AI solutions to streamline workflows, enhance productivity and support more data-driven decision-making," said technology expert Sam Goh. QC Capital operates as an AI-focused technology holding and venture-building platform spanning technology development, incubation and industrial investment.

items.title: KPJ strengthens preventive healthcare strategy
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: KPJ Healthcare Bhd is strengthening its focus on preventive healthcare with the launch of a nationwide campaign to raise awareness of digestive health and promote the early detection of gastrointestinal diseases. Under its Digestive Care 4 Life campaign, the hospital operator will provide 500 complimentary endoscopy and colonoscopy procedures to eligible patients referred by the Health Ministry facilities to participating KPJ hospitals nationwide. KPJ Healthcare president and managing director Chin Keat Chyuan said the campaign reflects the group's commitment to improving health outcomes through prevention and early intervention while reinforcing its role in advancing community health beyond hospital-based treatment.

items.title: Scientists discover 'hyperparasite' in Borneo jungle
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian scientists have discovered a new species of parasitic fungus in Borneo that preys on the "zombie fungus" known to kill insects. It was dubbed a "hyperparasite" because it "effectively parasitises the primary pathogen," said the institute's deputy director Jaya Seelan Sathiya Seelan. "The fungus belongs to the genus Pleurocordyceps and acts as a specialised hyperparasite," Seelan said.

items.title: PKR candidate list for Johor, NS state elections 99pct complete – Fuziah
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: PKR's list of candidates for the Johor and Negri Sembilan state elections is 99 per cent final, said party secretary-general Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh. She said PKR would contest 20 state seats in Johor and 16 in Negri Sembilan, with the official candidate announcement expected in the next few days. The Johor assembly has 56 seats.

items.title: Immigration DG: Actual number of refugees in Malaysia higher than UNHCR’s 200,000 figure
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — The Immigration Department today said the actual number of refugees in Malaysia is believed to be higher than the roughly 200,000 individuals currently recorded by the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR). According to Kosmo , Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said the government is carrying out a comprehensive registration exercise involving all refugees in the country, including those without UNHCR cards. Zakaria said this at a press conference after the passing-out parade for the 2026 Series 1 Basic Immigration and Higher Immigration Certificate courses at the Malaysian Immigration Academy in Port Dickson today.

items.title: Selangor hawkers to get six-month rent discount from July under state relief package
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 19 — The Selangor government will provide a 30 per cent rental reduction for hawkers and traders operating at Local Authority (PBT) premises under Phase Two of the Selangor Resilience Strengthening Package. “This assistance will benefit 21,234 traders with an allocation of RM4.5 million from the state government. Amirudin said that under the same package, the state government will also introduce a three-month moratorium for 1,012 tenants of the Selangor Smart Sewa scheme beginning in October.

items.title: Price volatility keeps India demand modest, China flips to discount
items.summary: Gold demand was modest in India this week as prices fell to their lowest level in two-and-a-half months and remained volatile, while top consumer China flipped to a discount for the first time since late December. Dealers in India quoted discounts of up to US$54 an ounce over official domestic prices this week, inclusive of 15 per cent import and 3 per cent sales levies, widening from last week's discounts of up to US$35. Domestic gold prices fell to 146,252 rupees per 10 grams on Friday, the lowest since April 2.

items.title: Bentong trailer driver freed on bail pending probe into fatal Karak Expressway crash [WATCH]
items.summary: PEKAN: The trailer driver involved in a fatal crash that killed four people in Bentong on June 14 has been released on police bail pending prosecution. Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said the driver is being investigated for causing death while driving under the influence of drugs and for drug consumption. "The driver's remand order, which began on Monday, expired yesterday (June 18), and he has since been released.

items.title: PKR to contest Puteri Wangsa state seat despite Johor Amanah claims
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: PKR will contest the Puteri Wangsa state seat in the upcoming Johor election despite state Amanah's claims over the seat. Recently, Johor Amanah issued a statement opposing the decision to hand over the Puteri Wangsa state seat to PKR for the upcoming state election. Johor Amanah vice-chairman Dr Zuhan Zain stated that the state chapter had never agreed to cede the seat.

items.title: Inflation in Malaysia edges up to 2.0pct, still one of Asia's lowest rates
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Inflation in Malaysia expanded to two per cent in May from 1.8 per cent in April, driven mainly by higher prices for communications, food and beverages, housing-related expenses and recreation. At 2.0 per cent, the country's inflation remained below that of the Philippines (6.8 per cent), Vietnam (5.6 per cent), South Korea (3.1 per cent), Indonesia (3.1 per cent) and Thailand (2.8 per cent), although it was higher than China's 1.2 per cent. The food and beverages category, which accounts for 29.8 per cent of the CPI basket, recorded faster growth of 1.4 per cent in May compared with 1.2 per cent in April.

items.title: Nine arrested in probe over fake medical certificates in Pekan
items.summary: PEKAN: Four more people have been arrested over the sale and purchase of fake medical certificates, bringing the total number of suspects to nine. Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said all nine suspects, who were previously remanded, will be released on police bail today. "The nine consist of a nurse, alleged middlemen, and several individuals believed to have purchased the fake certificates.

items.title: Pakatan top leaders will decide Puteri Wangsa seat dispute, says PKR sec-gen
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 19 — PKR and Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) leadership will hold talks soon to resolve a dispute over the Puteri Wangsa state seat ahead of the Johor state election. Yesterday, Amanah strongly objected to the Puteri Wangsa seat being handed over to PKR in the upcoming state election. He said Johor Amanah has made early preparations and has its machinery ready to reclaim and defend the Puteri Wangsa state seat.

items.title: As pigeon-munching croc settles near Kuching floating mosque, Sarawak minister urges forestry agency to act
items.summary: KUCHING, June 19 — Dato Sri Abdul Karim Rahman Hamzah has called on the Sarawak Forestry Corporation (SFC) to take immediate action following the sighting of a large crocodile in the Sarawak River near the Masjid India floating mosque. The Minister of Tourism, Creative Industry and Performing Arts acknowledged the complex situation, noting the crocodile’s presence carries a dual nature. “Well, we do know of the existence of the crocodiles in the Sarawak River, especially under the floating mosque.

items.title: PKR puts Rafizi, Nik Nazmi exit under legal review, Wong Chen faces disciplinary process
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 19 — PKR will refer any legal considerations involving former Pandan MP Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and former Setiawangsa MP Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad to its appointed legal team, while also passing a separate disciplinary matter involving Subang MP Wong Chen to the party’s disciplinary board. PKR secretary-general Datuk Fuziah Salleh said any follow-up action involving Rafizi and Nik Nazmi, who have both left the party, will be handled by party lawyers. “Both have already resigned and left the party.

items.title: Selangor expands RM100m financing scheme to help contractors weather supply crunch
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 19 — The Selangor state government plans to expand its Selangor Advance (SA500) programme to support the cash flow needs of companies involved in state infrastructure projects, including those undertaken at the district and local authority levels. “With an allocation of RM100 million, the initiative is expected to benefit 500 companies and contractors operating in Selangor,” he said while tabling the package at a special sitting of the Selangor State Legislative Assembly here today. Amirudin said the state government will also introduce interest-free financing of up to RM10,000 for micro, small and medium entrepreneurs through a collaboration between Yayasan Hijrah Selangor (Hijrah) and Platform Selangor (PLATS).

items.title: Sultan Ibrahim, in role as police honorary commissioner, receives security briefing from top cop
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today granted an audience to Inspector-General of Police (IGP) Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail at Istana Bukit Tunku here. According to a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar’s official Facebook page, the audience was held in His Majesty’s capacity as the Honorary Commissioner of the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM). During the audience, Sultan Ibrahim was briefed by Mohd Khalid on a range of matters relating to national security and public order.

items.title: PKR sec-gen confirms party candidates’ list ‘99pc’ ready for Johor, Negeri Sembilan polls
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 19 — PKR’s list of candidates for the upcoming Johor and Negeri Sembilan state elections is 99 per cent complete, party secretary-general Datuk Fuziah Salleh said today. She confirmed that PKR will contest 20 state seats in Johor and 16 in Negeri Sembilan under the Pakatan Harapan (PH) banner. The Negeri Sembilan state assembly was dissolved on June 5.

items.title: Sabah first: Ewon says Upko's GRS entry is about standing as strong as Sarawak
items.summary: KOTA BELUD, June 19 — United Progressive Kinabalu Organisation (Upko) President Datuk Ewon Benedick said the party must continue to play a role in strengthening unity and cooperation among Sabah-based political parties, in line with the spirit of “Sabah First”, which remains one of its core struggles. “Let us take the lead, and Kadamaian should contribute towards fostering unity and cooperation among Sabah’s local parties. This is what we mean by Sabah First,” he said.

items.title: Transport minister says JPJ has bigger road safety concerns than car sunshades
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — Transport Minister Anthony Loke today clarified that the Road Transport Department (JPJ) is not prioritising enforcement against vehicle sunshades, following public confusion over whether removable sunshades and curtains are allowed on car windows. According to Berita Harian , Loke said while existing rules allow action to be taken if such accessories breach road transport regulations, JPJ’s focus remains on offences that pose a more serious threat to road users. “There are many road offences, but there are more critical safety-related matters that are JPJ’s priority.

items.title: Hadi keeps mum on Perikatan turmoil, says Ahmad Samsuri’s statement is ‘enough’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang has declined to comment on current developments in Perikatan Nasional (PN), including the position of PAS and Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) in the coalition. According to Utusan Malaysia , the Marang MP said PN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar’s recent statement on the coalition was sufficient for now. “Samsuri’s statement is enough.

items.title: Anwar in Ashgabat for two-day official trip to boost Malaysia-Turkmenistan ties
items.summary: ASHGABAT (Turkmenistan), June 19 — After completing a two-day working visit to Kazan, Republic of Tatarstan, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim arrived here yesterday in the “White Marble City” for another leg of his visit to Central Asia. The aircraft carrying Anwar landed at Ashgabat International Airport at 8pm local time (11pm Malaysian time) for a two-day official visit from June 18-19. The trip marks Anwar’s first official visit to Turkmenistan and the fifth by a Malaysian prime minister.

items.title: Police probe suspected robbery after teen girl found injured in Tumpat
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 19 — A 15-year-old girl sustained serious injuries in a suspected robbery at Kampung Mesira, Palekbang, Tumpat, on Wednesday. “The victim was taken to Tumpat Hospital for initial treatment before being transferred to Raja Permaisuri Zainab II Hospital in Kota Bharu for further treatment by specialists,” he said in a statement yesterday. Mohd Khairi said initial investigations revealed that the victim was allegedly hit on the face and head with a stick by a suspect.

items.title: Reported missing from Johor at 15, woman now 21 found with child in Langkawi
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, June 19 — A woman who had been reported missing since 2019 after leaving her family home in Johor Bahru was found safe at a house in Taman Helang Perdana, Kuah, Wednesday. “Further checks based on missing persons records found that the woman was still listed as a missing person following a police report lodged in Majidee, Johor, in 2019. “The woman admitted to leaving her family home in Johor Bahru before settling in Langkawi.

items.title: Mara to place former soldiers as full-time wardens at all MRSM institutions from next January
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) will place full-time wardens from among former soldiers at all 58 Maktab Rendah Sains Mara (MRSM) nationwide from January 1, 2027. Mara chairman Datuk Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said the initiative would begin with 10 MRSM from July 1, after the selection and screening of candidates are carried out throughout this month. “A study by Universiti Putra Malaysia showed that the pilot project placing full-time wardens from among former soldiers at MRSM Besut and MRSM Balik Pulau had successfully improved student discipline and even achieved zero bullying,” he said in a Facebook post today.

items.title: Anwar praises Putin, Xi for condemning Gaza, Iran attacks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — Malaysian Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has commended Russian President Vladimir Putin and Chinese President Xi Jinping for advocating peace and condemning atrocities against the people of Gaza, Iran and Lebanon. Anwar said Malaysia has consistently taken a firm position against attacks by the Zionist regime while upholding its principles independently of any superpower. “We took a decision in Parliament, bipartisan, and continued to engage,” he said during an exclusive interview with Russian international television network RT.

items.title: Anwar pushes trade, AI and energy ties between Asean and Russia
items.summary: KAZAN, June 19 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged Asean and Russia to strengthen cooperation in various strategic areas, while stressing that dialogue and diplomacy must continue to be the foundation in handling conflicts and geopolitical tensions. Anwar also stressed the importance of forging closer cooperation in trade and investment, digital, science and technology, artificial intelligence (AI), energy, food security, the halal industry, and people-to-people exchanges to generate greater benefits for Asean countries and Russia. The two-day summit represents the highest platform for Asean and Russia to discuss strategic cooperation and exchange views on regional and international issues of mutual importance.

items.title: KTMB announces extra ETS trains for Johor voters, ticket sales start today
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 19 — Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) has announced additional ETS train services to cater to voters travelling home for the Johor state election. In a Facebook post, KTMB said that ticket sales will open at noon today and can be purchased via the KTMB Mobile app, the KITS Style app, the official website (online.ktmb.com.my), or at KTMB ticket kiosks. The Election Commission has set the polling day for the Johor state election on July 11, following the dissolution of the State Legislative Assembly on June 1.

items.title: Malaysia to ‘seriously’ explore using local currencies in bilateral trade, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — Malaysia will “seriously” explore using local currencies with trading partners to mitigate volatile foreign exchange risks, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. He said that Malaysia was already trading significantly with China and Russia, while India had agreed to start trading in its currency or undertake barter trade. Anwar, who is also the finance minister, cited the success of such bilateral payment arrangements in domestic currencies, such as with China.

items.title: ‘No excuse’ for poor maintenance, Hannah Yeoh says after complaints over Putrajaya facilities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — Federal Territories Minister Hannah Yeoh today said there should be “no excuse” for poor maintenance of public facilities, following complaints about broken lifts, faulty escalators and deteriorating infrastructure in Putrajaya. Yeoh said Putrajaya Corporation (PPj) had already begun carrying out repairs, but stressed that maintenance standards should be upheld consistently rather than only after public complaints gain traction online. Yeoh acknowledged that some facilities would require additional funding for upgrades but said local authorities should prioritise basic maintenance, cleanliness and safety.

items.title: DBKL says Sentul hawkers not being evicted, will return to site after RM1.6m upgrade completes in October
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) today sought to reassure traders and residents that the demolition of hawker structures opposite Mini UTC Sentul was part of a redevelopment project to upgrade the area, and not a permanent closure of hawker businesses there. “DBKL wishes to clarify that the demolition of hawker structures at Jalan 3/48A, UTC Sentul, which has gone viral on social media, is part of the redevelopment of the hawker area and is not intended to terminate or relocate hawkers’ business activities,” he said. According to DBKL records, the area comprises 33 hawker structures, of which 20 are still actively operating.

items.title: Why Muslim marriages break down in Malaysia: Here’s what SIS Forum’s 10-year data shows
items.summary: ARA DAMANSARA, June 18 — The top three reasons why Muslim couples’ marriages broke down in Malaysia over the past 10 years were communication problems, domestic violence and infidelity, a 10-year report by Muslim advocacy group SIS Forum (Malaysia) has shown. Based on SIS Forum (Malaysia)’s latest report released yesterday, a total of 4,761 persons (about 90 per cent women) had approached its free legal advice service Telenisa in the past decade from 2016 to 2025, with 3,438 of those cases involving marriage breakdowns. In the report, SIS Forum said communication problems peaked at 166 cases in 2018 and remained high in subsequent years, while domestic violence and infidelity both peaked in 2021.

items.title: Cabinet approves National Tahfiz Council, Zahid to chair new body
items.summary: KUANTAN, June 19 — The federal government has approved the establishment of the National Tahfiz Council, marking a major step in streamlining the direction, education model, recognition standards and academic pathways for tahfiz students nationwide. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the approval was given at last week’s Cabinet meeting. Meanwhile, the Deputy Prime Minister expressed hope that the establishment of the National Tahfiz Council would position tahfiz education as a recognised educational pathway within the education system.

items.title: Tengku Zafrul eyes Selangor seat in GE16, points to Pandan vacancy and Ampang groundwork
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — Senior Political Adviser to the Prime Minister, Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Tengku Abdul Aziz, has proposed that he be given the opportunity to contest a seat in Selangor in the 16th General Election (GE16). He said the proposal had been submitted to the PKR leadership for consideration and approval by the party’s president, Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Previously, former Economy Minister Rafizi Ramli announced his departure from PKR and vacated the Pandan parliamentary seat.
